# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent elasticsearch exposed apis.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 

xpack:
   security:
     enabled: true # Enable XPack Security features if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     http:
       enabled: true # Enable HTTP-based security for all Elasticsearch endpoints if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     authc: # Enable authentication for all Elasticsearch endpoints if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     user_name: "elastic" # The default username for HTTP-based security if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'elastic'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current user name it will be used as read only.
     password: "changeme" # The default password for HTTP-based security if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'password'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current user name it will be used as read only.
     roles: # List of users with their respective role(s) for HTTP-based security if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is empty list unless explicitly set to 'user'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     realm: "Elasticsearch" # The default Realm for HTTP-based security if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'Realm'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current realm it will be used as read only.
     cors: # Enable CORS for all Elasticsearch endpoints if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     allowed_origins: ["*"] # The list or pattern for CORS headers if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '*.elasticsearch.org'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current allowed origins it will be used as read only.
     allow_headers: ["*"] # The list or pattern for CORS headers if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '*.elasticsearch.org'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current allowed origins it will be used as read only.
     allow_methods: ["GET", "PUT"] # The list or pattern for CORS headers if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '*.elasticsearch.org'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current allowed origins it will be used as read only.
     expose_headers: ["*"] # The list or pattern for CORS headers if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '*.elasticsearch.org'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current allowed origins it will be used as read only.
     allow_credentials: true # Whether or not credentials are sent with requests from browsers, etc., if you want full control over your cluster's permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     credentials_expire: 3600 # The number seconds after which a user's session will expire if they use HTTP-based security and their password changes, etc., If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions and access controls including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 3600.
     trusted_external: false # Whether to trust the external IP addresses if they are not within a whitelist, or only allow connections from certain internal networks with specific CIDRs and/or subnets defined in your network settings file(elasticsearch-networks). Default is 'false'. When this option has been set to true it will make Elasticsearch trust all external IP addresses.
     trusted_internal: false # Whether or not connections from certain internal networks with specific CIDRs and/or subnets defined in your network settings file are allowed, if you want full control over the cluster's permissions for access controls including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'false'. When this option has been set to true it will allow connections from certain internal networks with specific CIDRs and/or subnets defined in your network settings file.
     http_basic: # Enable HTTP Basic authentication for all Elasticsearch endpoints if you want full control over the cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     username: "elastic" # The default user for HTTP Basic authentication if you want full control over your cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'username', when set to a value other than current it will be used as read only and not displayed.
     password: "changeme" # The default user for HTTP Basic authentication if you want full control over your cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'password', when set to a value other than current it will be used as read only and not displayed.
     realm: "Elasticsearch" # The default Realm for HTTP Basic authentication if you want full control over your cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'Realm', when set to a value other than current it will be used as read only and not displayed.
     authz_user: # Enable authorization for all Elasticsearch endpoints if you want full control over your cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is false unless explicitly set to 'true'. When this option has been disabled the following settings are not available.
     authz_realm: "Elasticsearch" # The default Realm for authorization if you want full control over your cluster's permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'Realm', when set to a value other than current it will be used as read only and not displayed.
     authz_allow: true # Whether or not authorization should allow access if no user was found for the request, etc., If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'true'. When this option has been set to false it will deny all requests.
     authz_deny: true # Whether or not authorization should allow access if no user was found for the request, etc., If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'true'. When this option has been set to false it will deny all requests.
     transport_audit: # Enable audit logging if enabled for network traffic, etc., If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions and access controls including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'false'. When this option has been set to true it will enable auditing.
     transport_audit_log: # The log file path if enabled for network traffic audit logging, etc., If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions and access controls including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '/var/log/{cluster}/transport-audit.log'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current log file path it will be used as read only, not displayed or editable by users and can't change once enabled for transport audit logging if disabled previously with 'false', when explicitly set false otherwise user cannot enable/disable these settings in yaml.
     http_trace: # Enable HTTP tracing (enable request-response data collection) If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions, including encryption of data at rest using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is 'false'. When this option has been set to true it will enable the feature.
     http_trace_log: # The log file path if enabled for HTTP tracing logging If you want full control over your cluster’s permissions and access controls, including encryption of data at rest in transit using Transport Layer Security (TLS). Default is '/var/log/{cluster}/http-trace.log'. When this option has been set to a value other than the current log file path it will be used as read only or not displayed by users can't change once enabled for HTTP tracing logging if disabled previously with 'false', when explicitly set false otherwise user cannot enable/disable these settings in yaml
